title: Model Kits Production Model and Limited Model
display_title: Model Kits Production Model and Limited Model
wikitext:
[]: Model Kits âÂ Production Model and Limited Model             Quote Post by e3ndgame » Mon Feb 09, 2015 10:30 pm So I'm a more recent EVA fan... and I'm really interested in buying a model kit. Problem is, I'm confused as hell when it comes to purchasing one. I've been looking around on eBay to import to Canada, and I've found two model I like, but don't complete understand what the difference is between them. I've looked online and can't seem to find a concrete answer to my question: what is the main difference between a Production Model and Limited Model Kit ? These are the two kits I've been looking at: http://www.ebay.ca/itm/251569194096?_trksid=p2055119.m1438.l2649&ssPageName=STRK%3AMEBIDX%3AIT http://www.ebay.ca/itm/400567222608?_trksid=p2055119.m1438.l2649&ssPageName=STRK%3AMEBIDX%3AIT If anyone is able to provide a concrete explanation, please do. I understand I am completely new at this and need the answers from the serious collectors.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Feb 09, 2015 11:05 pm "Limited Model" is a line Bandai had in the 90s where you had very few simple parts for very cheap and jack shit poseability. It's one of the few lines of kits that Bandai stopped making because of how crummy they are. The first kit is a crummy, small (16cm tall) Limited Model. Because it's no long produced some people try to use this excuse to jack up the price because it's rare. IMO these kits are completely worthless. The second kit is the much better and a bit bigger (20cm tall) Limited Model High Grade . The Production Model is part of the Eva-03 name (and not the model type), since it is a Production Model Evangelion like Eva-02. Bandai still makes these kits on a regular enough basis and they are quite nice by 90s HG standards. IMO they are the a very lovely representation of the original Evas as far as proportions go. You can find more detailed pics and manuals scans of it here: http://www.1999.co.jp/eng/10014553 It is important to note that with LMHG model kits for Eva-03 have the Eva-03 version (normal Eva) and Bardiel version (long, lanky arms) The LM model for 03 only comes as Bardiel with long, lanky arms. EDIT: As far as shipping to Canada goes, you might save some money if you wait a bit until Bandai does another run of the LMHG kits and buy it from a Japanese retailer. Amiami has it for only 1,834 and it should be around another 1,000-1,300 yen for SAL shipping, so definitely cheaper than $56CAD from ebay. http://www.amiami.com/top/detail/detail?gcode=GDS-2972E

[]:             Quote Post by e3ndgame » Tue Feb 10, 2015 9:43 pm Perfect. Thank you! Make's perfect sense now.
